title: cotula nigellifolia
intro: Cotula nigellifolia, also known as African bush marigold, is a flowering plant species native to South Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make herbal preparations like infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which have been found to have health benefits including reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. Additionally, cotula nigellifolia has been traditionally used to treat conditions like eczema, acne, and dermatitis due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
